A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire — it began teaching in 1096.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the building of the Great Pyramid.
Woolly mammoths were still alive when the Great Pyramid was being built.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
Honey never spoils — edible honey has been found in 3,000-year-old Egyptian tombs.
The shortest war in history lasted 38–45 minutes (Anglo-Zanzibar War, 1896).
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
A snail can sleep for three years.
The total weight of all ants on Earth is greater than the total weight of all humans.
Bananas are technically berries, but strawberries are not.
Wombats produce cube-shaped droppings — the only animal known to do so.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
Humans share 50% of their DNA with bananas.
The average cloud weighs around 500,000 kg.
Crows can recognise and remember human faces, and hold grudges.
A day on Mars is 24 hours and 37 minutes — very close to an Earth day.
The Eiffel Tower grows about 15cm taller in summer due to thermal expansion.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
The human nose can detect over one trillion different scents.
A group of owls is called a parliament.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way.
The shortest commercially available flight lasts under 2 minutes (Westray to Papa Westray, Scotland).
Sharks are older than trees — sharks appeared ~450 million years ago, trees ~350 million years ago.
Finland has more saunas than cars.
A day on Mercury lasts longer than a year on Mercury.
The average person walks about 100,000 miles in their lifetime — roughly four times around the Earth.
Caterpillars completely dissolve into a liquid soup inside their cocoons before reforming as butterflies.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they don't drift apart.
The mantis shrimp can punch with the force of a bullet and see 16 types of colour receptors (humans have 3).
Penguins propose to their mates with a pebble.
A jiffy is an actual unit of time: 1/100th of a second.
The entire internet weighs about 50 grams — the weight of the electrons in motion.
Cows have best friends and become stressed when separated from them.
A group of porcupines is called a prickle.
Glass is technically a supercooled liquid, not a solid.
The longest recorded flight of a chicken is 13 seconds.
Goats have rectangular pupils.
The average person produces enough saliva in their lifetime to fill two swimming pools.
Trees in a forest communicate and share nutrients through underground fungal networks — the 'Wood Wide Web'.
There are more public libraries in the US than McDonald's restaurants.
A cockroach can live for weeks without its head — it dies of thirst eventually.
The voice actors of Mickey and Minnie Mouse were married in real life.
Polar bear fur is not white — each hair is transparent and hollow.
The Hawaiian alphabet has only 13 letters.
All clownfish are born male; the dominant one in a group changes to female.
The average human body contains enough iron to make a nail about 8cm long.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The dot over the letter 'i' is called a tittle.
A group of cats is called a clowder.
The moon is slowly drifting away from Earth at about 3.8cm per year.
The average person spends six months of their life waiting at red lights.
Elephants are the only animals that cannot jump.
A day on Pluto lasts about 6.4 Earth days.
The smell of freshly cut grass is actually a distress signal released by the grass.
Armadillos always give birth to identical quadruplets.
There is a species of jellyfish that is biologically immortal — it can revert to its juvenile state.
The total surface area of human lungs is roughly the size of a tennis court.
A group of crows is called a murder.
Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins — up to 40 minutes.
The hashtag symbol (#) is technically called an octothorpe.
Koalas have fingerprints almost identical to humans.
A flock of ravens is called an unkindness or a conspiracy.
The tongue is the fastest healing part of the human body.
There are more possible ways to shuffle a deck of cards than seconds that have elapsed since the Big Bang.
Tardigrades (water bears) can survive in outer space, extreme radiation and temperatures near absolute zero.
The Twitter bird logo is officially named Larry, after basketball player Larry Bird.
A group of pugs is called a grumble.
Bees can recognise human faces.
The moon has moonquakes — caused by Earth's gravitational pull.
A group of jellyfish is called a smack.
The world's quietest room, at Microsoft's headquarters, is so silent you can hear your own heartbeat and organs.
A group of hedgehogs is called an array.
Humans are the only animals that blush.
The Atlantic Ocean is growing wider by about 2.5cm per year.
Pistachios are technically a fruit, not a nut.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye — this is a myth.
A group of lemurs is called a conspiracy.
The odds of being born are roughly 1 in 400 trillion.
Squirrels forget where they buried about half their nuts — accidentally planting thousands of trees.
You cannot hum while holding your nose closed.
A group of kangaroos is called a mob.
The Hawaiian pizza was invented in Canada.
A group of sharks is called a shiver.
The only letter not appearing in any US state name is Q.
Ants never sleep — they take hundreds of micro-naps each day instead.
A group of giraffes is called a tower.
The tallest recorded wave in history was 524 metres tall — in Lituya Bay, Alaska, in 1958.
A group of penguins in water is called a raft.
Fortune cookies were invented in California, not China.
The world's oldest living organism is a bristlecone pine tree in California, estimated to be over 5,000 years old.
A group of parrots is called a pandemonium.
Hot water can freeze faster than cold water in certain conditions — the Mpemba effect.
A group of rhinos is called a crash.
Humans are the only primates that don't have pigmented eyes.
The first computer bug was an actual bug — a moth trapped in a relay of the Harvard Mark II in 1947.
A group of vultures circling in the sky is called a kettle.
There are more possible sudoku grids than there are grains of sand on Earth.
A group of ferrets is called a business.
The average person walks past 36 murderers in their lifetime — statistically.
Apples belong to the rose family.
A group of hippos is called a bloat.
A strawberry is not a berry, but a banana is.
Sloths only poo once a week, and lose up to a third of their body weight doing it.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
Lobsters were once so plentiful in North America that they were fed to prisoners — considered peasant food.
A group of cobras is called a quiver.
Humans are the only animals that cook their food.
The first text message ever sent said 'Merry Christmas'.
A group of wolves is called a pack, but a group of wolf pups is called a litter.
The brain uses about 20% of the body's total energy despite being only 2% of its mass.
A day on Uranus lasts about 17 Earth hours, but a year lasts 84 Earth years.
The average lifespan of a taste bud is 10 days.
There are more bacteria in the human mouth than people on Earth.
A group of meerkats is called a gang or a mob.
Blind people see in their dreams — though those blind from birth dream in sound, touch and emotion.
The surface area of Russia is slightly larger than the surface area of Pluto.
A group of otters is called a romp.
The original name for a butterfly was 'flutterby'.
An octopus can unscrew a jar from the inside.
A group of bats is called a cauldron.
The human eye can detect a candle flame from 48 kilometres away in complete darkness.
Walnuts look like miniature brains — and they are one of the best brain foods.
A group of baboons is called a troop or a congress.
There have been more people on the Moon than have swum to the deepest point of the ocean.
A group of crows on the ground is called a murder, but in flight it's called a horde.
The total length of blood vessels in the human body could circle Earth about 2.5 times.
Jellyfish have no brains, hearts, blood, or bones.
A group of hummingbirds is called a charm.
Strawberries are members of the rose family and are the only fruit with seeds on the outside.
Humans share 70% of their DNA with a slug.
A group of frogs is called an army.
Saturn's rings are made mostly of water ice and are only about 10 metres thick on average.
The average person produces about 25,000 quarts of saliva in a lifetime.
A group of peacocks is called a muster or an ostentation.
Dogs have a sense of time — they can tell how long you've been away.
A group of dolphins is called a pod.
The first oranges were not orange — early cultivated oranges were green.
A group of pigs is called a drift or a sounder.
There are 62 miles of bookshelves in the British Library.
A group of crows is called a murder — a single crow seen near a window is considered an omen in many cultures.
Tomatoes are a fruit in botanical terms but legally a vegetable in the USA after an 1893 Supreme Court ruling.
A group of stingrays is called a fever.
The average raindrop falls at about 9.5 kilometres per hour.
All giant pandas on Earth are technically on loan from China.
A group of foxes is called a skulk or an earth.
The elephant is the only mammal that cannot jump.
A group of crows gathering is called a congress.
The letter 'e' is the most commonly used letter in the English language.
A group of swans is called a bevy or a wedge in flight.
Turtles can breathe through their backsides.
A group of bears is called a sleuth.
The word 'galaxy' comes from the Greek word for milk — 'gala'.
A group of gorillas is called a troop.
Lightning strikes Earth about 100 times per second — or 8 million times per day.
A group of hyenas is called a cackle.
There is a species of ant (Dracula ant) that can snap its jaws shut 5,000 times faster than you can blink.
A group of eagles is called a convocation.
The first alarm clock could only ring at 4am — invented by Levi Hutchins in 1787.
A group of butterflies is called a kaleidoscope.
No two tigers have the same stripe pattern — like human fingerprints.
A group of wasps is called a colony or a pledge.
The longest English word that can be typed using only the left hand on a QWERTY keyboard is 'stewardesses'.
A group of mosquitoes is called a scourge.
The average human head weighs about 5 kilograms.
A group of crabs is called a consortium or a cast.
Some turtles can live over 100 years — the oldest recorded was 188.
A group of goldfish is called a troubling.
Avocados are toxic to most animals — but perfectly safe for humans.
A group of alligators is called a congregation.
Every human being started life as a single cell the size of a full stop.
A group of cheetahs is called a coalition.
The Canary Islands are named after dogs (canis), not canary birds.
A group of polar bears is called a pack.
The Twitter fail whale image became so iconic it has a Wikipedia page.
A group of crows at roost is called a murder.
There are 293 ways to make change for a US dollar.
A group of swallows is called a flight or a gulp.
The unicorn is the national animal of Scotland.
A group of mongooses is called a mob or a pack.
A cubic metre of fog contains less than half a cup of water.
A group of lizards is called a lounge.
There is a town in Norway called Hell — and it does indeed freeze over in winter.
A group of elephants is called a herd or a memory.
The stapes, a tiny bone in the human ear, is the smallest bone in the body.
A group of beavers is called a colony.
The average person farts 14 times per day — enough to fill a balloon.
A group of finches is called a charm.
Honey badgers have been documented escaping from enclosures by making and using tools.
A group of lions is called a pride.
The Amazon River discharges about 20% of all fresh water that flows into the world's oceans.
A group of deer is called a herd.
The moon is 400 times smaller than the Sun but also 400 times closer to Earth, creating a perfect eclipse.
A group of turtles is called a bale.
There are 10 times more bacterial cells in your body than human cells.
A group of ravens at rest is called an unkindness.
Crows are one of the few animals that make their own tools.
A group of leeches is called a bundle.
The footprints left by Apollo astronauts on the Moon will last for millions of years — no wind or rain to erode them.
A group of trout is called a hover.
It is physically impossible to lick your elbow — most people try immediately on reading this.
A group of hares is called a drove or a down.
The longest word in English with all letters in alphabetical order is 'almost'.
A group of moles is called a labour.
There are 118 elements in the periodic table — only 94 occur naturally.
A group of swans on the ground is called a herd.
Bananas are slightly radioactive due to their potassium-40 content.
A group of barracudas is called a battery.
The shortest sentence in English that uses all 26 letters is 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog'.
A group of iguanas is called a mess.
Dolphins have been documented giving each other names — unique whistles used to call a specific individual.
A group of sparrows is called a host.
Your eyes are the only organs in your body that don't grow from birth — they are the same size as when you were born.
A group of bison is called a herd or an obstinacy.
The shortest scheduled airline flight in the world is 1.7 miles, between Westray and Papa Westray in Scotland.
A group of nightingales is called a watch.
Bats always turn left when exiting a cave.
A group of monkeys is called a troop or a barrel.
The odds of drawing a royal flush in poker are 1 in 649,740.
A group of ducks in flight is called a skein.
Sloths move so slowly that algae grows on their fur — acting as camouflage.
A group of toucans is called a durante.
Most car horns beep in the key of F.
A group of snakes is called a nest or a pit.
The Great Barrier Reef can be seen from outer space.
A group of magpies is called a parliament or a tiding.
Chewing gum while cutting onions can prevent you from crying.
A group of otters in water is called a raft.
A group of starlings flying in formation is called a murmuration.
The moon orbits Earth at roughly 3,700 km/h.
A group of hedgehogs is called an array or a prickle.
The first photograph ever taken was taken in 1826 or 1827 by Joseph Nicéphore Niépce.
A group of toads is called a knot.
Female lions do about 90% of the hunting for the pride.
A group of giraffes moving together is called a journey.
The human skeleton is completely replaced every 10 years.
A group of quail is called a covey.
The strongest muscle in the human body relative to its size is the masseter — the jaw muscle.
A group of cows is called a herd — but a group of cats is called a clowder.
The average person blinks 15–20 times per minute — about 10 million times a year.
A group of crickets is called an orchestra.
The longest mountain range on Earth is underwater — the Mid-Ocean Ridge stretches over 65,000 km.
A group of turkeys is called a rafter or a flock.
A teaspoon of neutron star material would weigh about 900 times the mass of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
A group of capybaras is called a herd.
Plants can recognise their siblings and give preferential treatment in terms of root space.
A group of jellyfish is called a bloom or a swarm.
The inventor of the frisbee was turned into a frisbee after death — his ashes were moulded into one.
A group of porcupines is called a prickle.
There are more ways to arrange a deck of 52 cards than there have been seconds since the Big Bang.
A group of macaws is called a flock.
The moon is drifting away from Earth — in 600 million years, total solar eclipses will no longer occur.
A group of herrings is called an army.
Norway is so far north that during summer the sun never fully sets for months.
A group of parakeets is called a flock or a company.
Identical twins do not have identical fingerprints.
A group of boars is called a sounder.
The blue whale's heart is so large, a human could crawl through its aorta.
A group of kangaroos is called a mob or a troop.
Only two countries use purple in their national flags: Nicaragua and Dominica.
A group of newts is called a knot.
The speed of light is exactly 299,792,458 metres per second — exactly, because the metre is now defined by it.
A group of crocodiles on land is called a bask.
Humans are the only animals that have chins.
A group of ostriches is called a flock.
More Monopoly money is printed each year than real money.
A group of manta rays is called a squadron.
The left side of the brain controls the right side of the body and vice versa.
A group of mackerel is called a school.
The oldest known recipe is for beer — dating from ancient Mesopotamia, around 1800 BC.
A group of greyhounds is called a pack.
There is a type of tree (the manchineel tree) so toxic that standing under it in the rain can blister your skin.
A group of beetles is called a swarm.
Your heart will beat about 3 billion times in your lifetime.
A group of seals is called a pod or a colony.
The Hawaiian word for white-footed mouse — 'iole huna loa' — is one of the longest Hawaiian words.
A group of dragonflies is called a swarm.
Russia has 11 time zones — more than any other country.
A group of flamingos in flight is called a stand.
The first domain name ever registered was Symbolics.com on 15 March 1985.
A group of tuna is called a school.
The brain generates enough electricity to power a small light bulb.
A group of rhinos is called a crash.
Water is the only substance that exists naturally in all three states on Earth's surface.
A group of goldfinches is called a charm.
The Amazon rainforest produces about 20% of the world's oxygen.
A group of vipers is called a nest.
The word 'nerd' was first used in a Dr. Seuss book in 1950.
A group of red pandas is called an embarrassment.
The surface area of the Sahara Desert is comparable to that of the United States.
A group of kangaroo rats is called a mob.
There are 6 sexillion tonnes of Earth — that's 6 followed by 21 zeros.
A group of warthogs is called a sounder.
The human brain has about 86 billion neurons.
A group of starfish is called a constellation.
Snow is actually translucent, not white — it appears white because of how light scatters through it.
A group of emus is called a mob.
The Pacific Ocean is larger than all the world's land combined.
A group of cockroaches is called an intrusion.
The heart of a blue whale can weigh as much as a small car.
A group of bears is called a sloth.
Water expands by about 9% when it freezes — which is why ice floats.
A group of barracudas in motion is called a school.
The shortest commercial street in the world is Elgin Street in Bacup, Lancashire — just 17 feet long.
A group of locusts is called a plague.
The tongue print of every human is unique — as unique as a fingerprint.
A group of komodo dragons is called a den.
A human can survive longer without food than without sleep.
A group of hummingbirds at a feeder is called a hover.
Honey is made by bees regurgitating nectar repeatedly until the water content is low enough to resist fermentation.
A group of wolverines is called a pack.
There is enough gold in the Earth's core to coat the entire surface of the planet 1.5 metres deep.
A group of prairie dogs is called a town.
The platypus is one of only five species of mammals that lay eggs.
A group of lemurs is called a troop.
An average of 100 billion people have lived on Earth — you are one of them.
A group of mice is called a mischief.
